title: espeletia caldasii
intro: Espeletia caldasii, also known as the Caldas' tree or Queñoa, is a plant species native to the Andean region in South America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiparasitic effects. The health benefits of Espeletia caldasii include reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and treating parasitic infections such as giardiasis. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems are typically dried, ground into a powder, and then used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures.
